Petition For Order Relieving Admin J.L. N9ovak From Further Complaince With Terms Of Settlement Agreement And Etc.
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Jonas, for and with Julie Novak; Paul Melbostad, for and with Baird Bramer; Scott Anderson.

The Court asked if Mr. Bramer had a preference whether to receive the two guns or for them to be destroyed. Mr. Bramer responded that he wanted the guns, or in the alternative to have them destroyed and his attorneys fees be reimbursed to him. Mr. Jonas stated that there were two obligations on the part of the administrator under the settlement agreement: to make a payment to Mr. Bramer of $250,000, and to send him the two guns held in the estate. He further stated that the first obligation had been fully complied with, as the $250,000 payment was sent to Mr. Bramer and that he had deposited the funds, although he had failed to acknowledge receipt of the funds in writing as requested. As to the sending of the guns from their location in Minnesota to Mr. Bramer in California, Mr. Jonas said there were federal restrictions on the interstate transportation of weapons, and that Mr. Bramer had refused to fill in the rrequired paperwork for the transfer to go forward. He stated that as an alternative to shipping the guns, he had sent a check in the sum of $850 to Mr. Bramer, reflecting the appraised value of the guns, but that Mr. Bramer had refused to cash the check and demanded instead payment of $1500, which Mr. Jonas contended was a unilateral attempt on the part of Mr. Bramer to modify the terms of the settlement agreement. The Court asked if Mr. Bramer was willing to accept a payment in lieu of delivery of the guns. Mr. Bramer stated that he was, on the condition that the guns be turned in to the police in Minnesota for destruction. Mr. Jonas stated that Mr. Bramer had no right to compel destruction of a trust assets if he were compensated for it by receiving their equivalent value, and questioned whether the police in Minnesota had a program for surrender and destruction of firearms. Mr. Melbostad stated that when his father had passed away in Minnesota, he had been able to surrender his father's firearm to local police for destruction. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Broitman & Ann Asis for (administrator) Julie Novak. Paul Melbostad for and with Baird Bramer.

The Court asked Mr. Melbostad if they had filed written objections. Mr. Melbostad explained that it had been a surprise that the petition was pre-granted, that they had not notified Mr. Baird of such, that they do not object to the settlement but to how the settlement will be carried out, that they need instructions to the administrator and that the Minnesota Health Services should be given notice. Mr. Broitman stated that he does not know why we are here, that it is not known if there is a problem in Minnesota, that Ms. Wiebmer is not on public benefits and that Mr. Bramer is just a creditor in this estate. Mr. Melbostad stated that the concept of the agreement is that Mr. Bramer agreed that independent counsel be in place, that the court should be vigilant, that Ms. Wiebmer's life could be improved and that Mr. Bramer has personal information that she has had several surgeries and was on SSI. Mr. Broitman stated that that was 30 years ago, that they spent 20 hours on getting this agreement done, that they agreed to the administrative language, that the fiduciary would get independent counsel hired and that now they are trying to change what they agreed to. Mr. Melbostad stated that they are not seeking to have the language changed and read an excerpt from the document. Mr. Bramer reported that they are trying to avoid findings of fraud investigation in Minnesota, that lies have been told, that Ms. Wiebmer was told her share would be $17,000.00 and that she would lose her benefits, that Julie Novak does not have the capacity as an administrator to decide who counsel will be and that Social Workers and Psychiatrists are involved. Mr. Melbostad stated that there is a possibility of having her share go into a Special Needs Trust. Mr. Broitman stated that Eric Sofgee lied to the Minnesota administrator to get this going and that he will file a declaration to such, that the settlement agreement was signed as to how it would go forward and that there was no undue influence or fraud. Mr. Melbostad stated that Julie Novak has blatant economic interest. The Court took the petition for order approving settlement agreement and mutual release under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
